entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
You have it: 

  • IF_SXML_READER/IF_SXML_WRITER with IF_SXML~CO_XT_JSON (used in XCO_JSON)
  • IF_JSON_READER/IF_JSON_WRITER (I think it is available from SAP_BASIS 8.16)

It will parse any JSON in the kernel. But getting it mapped into ABAP data is another story. The kernel does not know all ABAP types and data structures. And the creation of complex data structures shall be done in ABAP memory, but not in the kernel.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Business Rules: Understanding Modulo Function
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’ve decided to share some tips on hidden functions within SuccessFactors Business Rules that often go underutilized. Using these functions can help clean up your logic, reduce the number of complex 'If' conditions, and ultimately improve system performance.

Today, we’re focusing on the Modulo() function. Simply put, Modulo() returns the remainder of a number after division. For a deeper dive, check out the Implementing Business Rules in SAP SuccessFactors document in the Help Portal.

For a better understanding I will cover two scenarios.

 

Scenario 1: Validating Full-Week Absences

In this case, we want to ensure an absence request covers exactly one or more full weeks. For example, in some organizations, employees are requested to take sabbatical in full week at a time. Here is how you can set this up:

 Image from Demo

 

In this logic, we check if the remainder of the total absence duration divided by 7 is anything other than zero. If the result is 0, the absence is exactly one or more full weeks. If the result is not 0, it indicates the employee is not taking the full week.

 

Scenario 2: Eligibility Every Four Years

In the beginning of my consulting days, I encountered a specific requirement: a company policy granting employees four weeks of sabbatical every four years. This means eligibility triggers at the 4th, 8th, and 12th years of seniority, and so on.

Rather than creating a separate 'If' condition for every possible seniority level, you can streamline the entire requirement using the Modulo() function:

Image from Demo

 

A remainder of zero indicates the seniority year is a multiple of four, confirming the employee’s eligibility for sabbatical. If the remainder is any other value, they are not yet eligible.

 

I hope this information has been helpful and encourages you to explore our standard functions to streamline your own implementation projects! As always, please discuss with your customers the pros and cons of adopting such a function in your projects.

entry.title: Preparing for SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release – Universal Full Profile View
entry.content:
With the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release, the Latest People Profile – Full Profile View will become universally available for all customers. As part of this change, the enablement status will shift from Customer Configured to Automatically On, meaning Full Profile View will be enabled by default and no longer controlled by a system setting.

While the Full Profile View will be turned on automatically, customers must still complete required configuration activities to ensure the experience is aligned with their business needs.

Action is required prior to the 1H 2026 universal push. Join this session to understand what preparation is required, what typically comes up as asks and use the chance to ask questions.

Customer Registration required: Microsoft Virtual Events Powered by Teams

This event is limited to 1000 seats. If you cannot attend, the recording and presentation will be posted on the customer community blog: customer community blog.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud, on AVC Need to derive BOM quantity based on characteristic calculation
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Hello,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC).

Business requirement:
The finished product has a numeric characteristic Z_LONGITUD (unit: M).
This value should determine the component quantity of a cable material in the BOM (unit of measure: M), so that:

Configured Length → Component Quantity in BOM → Correct MRP Planning

What I have tried:

  • Set the BOM item category to Variable-Size Item (R)

  • Defined one size dimension (Length, unit M)

  • Created a formula key (e.g. ROMS1)

  • Attempted to assign ROMS1 = $ROOT.Z_LONGITUD in a procedure dependency → syntax error

  • Attempted to use constraint logic to influence quantity → not allowed

  • Variable-size item does not seem to allow direct mapping to a configurable characteristic in this tenant

Question:

Is it technically supported in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to dynamically derive the BOM component quantity from a configurable characteristic (numeric, continuous value)?

If yes:

  • What is the correct modeling approach?

  • Should this be done via Variable-Size Item reference characteristic?

  • Is Quantity Formula supported?

If not

  • It is side-by-side extensibility (BTP) required?

The goal is to ensure correct MRP planning based on the configured length.

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: How to hide "Compensation Structure" object for some...
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 ,

If you're trying to hide the field during a hire or rehire process, please use rule scenario 'onInit Rules for Hire/Rehire'. Using an onInit rule for an existing employee will not work, as onInit rules apply only during Hire/Rehire scenarios. As an alternative, you could create a dedicated permission role for the HR team in that country(giving permission to this field) or configure an error message for other countries.

Regards,

VR

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01: Final What's New & Product Assistance
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final version of What's New information and Product Assistance for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 is now available:

  • To access the final version of the Product Assistance on the current SAP Help Portal pages, please use SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.
  • To access the final What’s New information, please use What's New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA or What's New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

For any feedback you may have, you can use the commenting feature for What's New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the SAP Help Portal. The feature is only available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

In addition, you have access to further content, for example, the conversion and upgrade guides specifically tailored for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Please refer to the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition pages for more details

entry.title: Engage with SAP Online: The Global Event You Can’t Afford to Miss
entry.content:
On March 11, 2026, the world’s leading marketing, CX, and loyalty innovators will gather for Engage with SAP Online - a high‑impact, full‑day virtual experience designed to help brands close the engagement gap and build customer relationships that actually last. Streaming live at 9:00 a.m. ET / 1:00 p.m. GMT / 2:00 p.m. CET, this event brings together the thinkers, practitioners, and trendsetters shaping the future of customer engagement.

If you’re responsible for growth, loyalty, CX, or digital transformation, this is your front‑row seat to the strategies that will define 2026 and beyond.

 

🌍What to Expect: A Day of Unfiltered Insight

A Global Lineup of Industry Leaders

Hear directly from the experts who are cutting through the noise and delivering real results. Expect practical guidance, honest conversations, and frameworks you can put into action immediately.

Featured speakers include:

  • Mark Ritson, globally recognized marketing and brand strategist

  • Essity and Sinch, joining Mark for a panel on modernizing CX with AI

  • Jutta Richter, Head of 1:1 Campaign Management, BMW Group

  • Senior leaders from SAP and top global brands

Expect honest conversations, sharp perspectives, and frameworks you can put into practice immediately.

These aren’t scripted presentations - they’re candid discussions about what’s working, what’s not, and what’s coming next.

 

📅Agenda Highlights

Welcome & Opening Insights

Kick off the day with a deep dive into the SAP Engagement Index, revealing where brands are meeting customer expectations and where they’re falling behind.

The Trends That Actually Matter (with Mark Ritson)

AI‑powered consumers are moving faster than brands can react. Mark Ritson breaks down:

  • Which CX trends are real

  • How influence shapes behavior

  • Why loyalty is now a cross‑functional responsibility

Expect sharp takes, myth‑busting, and actionable clarity.

Tech, Trust & Real‑World CX Wins

A panel of global leaders explores how AI, modernized systems, and connected experiences are driving measurable business impact today.

The Power of Influence in Customer Journeys

From creators to communities, discover how influence shapes discovery, trust, and loyalty long before your brand enters the conversation.

Closing Takeaways

Wrap up with the key themes and next steps you can bring back to your team, including where to focus now to close the Engagement Divide.

 

💡Why This Event Matters

This isn’t another virtual conference filled with slides and buzzwords. It’s a global pulse check on how customer expectations are shifting — and how leading brands are adapting with speed, intelligence, and authenticity.

Attendees will walk away with:

  • Practical frameworks for modern engagement

  • AI‑driven strategies that scale

  • Real examples of brands creating measurable impact

  • A global network of CX and marketing leaders

If you’re serious about elevating your customer engagement strategy, this is the event that will shape your roadmap for the year ahead.

 

📣Why You Should Invite Your Customers

Engage with SAP Online gives your customers direct access to the insights, strategies, and innovations that will help them grow. By inviting them, you’re offering:

  • A look at what’s driving loyalty and engagement right now

  • Access to global experts and future‑ready brands

  • Actionable takeaways they can implement immediately

It’s a value‑add that strengthens your relationship and positions you as a partner invested in their success.

 

🚀Don’t Miss Out — Register Now

Customer expectations are evolving faster than ever. Brands that adapt will thrive. Brands that don’t will fall behind.

Secure your spot and invite your customers to Engage with SAP Online. This is your chance to get ahead of the trends shaping 2026 and redefine how your brand connects, engages, and grows!

entry.title: Re: Change from MsgType FIDCC2 to ACC_DOCUMENT
entry.content:
Activities Required

Partner Profile Update (WE20)
Define outbound parameters for message type ACC_DOCUMENT with basic type ACC_DOCUMENT04. Remove or deactivate FIDCC2 outbound settings.

Distribution Model (BD64)
Adjust ALE distribution model to send ACC_DOCUMENT instead of FIDCC2. Assign the correct logical systems.

Process Code Assignment (WE42)
Ensure process code for ACC_DOCUMENT is active and points to the correct function module (ACC_DOCUMENT_POST).

Treasury System Outbound
Update the sending application (Treasury) to trigger ACC_DOCUMENT IDocs instead of FIDCC2. This usually means switching the message type in customizing or interface config.

Testing
Run end‑to‑end test: create posting in Treasury → IDoc generated → received in S/4 FI → posted successfully. Validate that no enrichment is required (segments map directly).

entry.title: Error F5428 Balance in 1 currencies during activity SLP in SIL for project settlement AuC
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are implementing a new tax ledger using the "Subsequent Implementation of a further Accounting Principle" tool (Release SAPK-10704INS4CORE).

During execution of activity SLP in SIL Cockpit we are facing error F5428 Balance in 1 currencies:

Balances have been found in 1 currencies with the following details:

Currency type Amount Currency
30
6.552,18-
EUR

The balance comes from a ledger group specific posting for project settlement against assets under construction and the missing amount comes from the line item posting to the AuC. The amount in that line is exactly 6.552,18 so it is as if the currency type 30 is missing completely only for that line item to balance the document out (so no FX issue).
There is also an equivalent posting in IFRS as the non-leading ledger in the system but this are not relevant for datatransfer in SIL)

Does anyone have any experience where this might come from?

High level scope of implementation of tax ledger:
1 company code on system
Source ledger = local GAAP (which is also leading ledger, IFRS is non-leading ledger)
One depreciation area in new tax ledger as source ledger only has one depreciation area posting in real time, rest are statistical

FIN General Ledger #Investment Management #SIL #SLP

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: Joule-Powered Migration, Datasphere Insights and How to find your community!
entry.content:
In this edition of SAP Community Voice, we bring you a mix of innovation, technical deep dives, and inspiring community stories. From AI-driven custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule, to an in-depth exploration of SAP Datasphere's Object Store in Business Data Cloud, this week's highlights are packed with insights to help you stay ahead.

Beyond technology, we also celebrate the human side of our community, including an inspiring Ironman journey in our Hobbies Series, that proves resilience applies both in sports and in SAP projects. Plus, we have updates on the new CEI project for SAP Integration Suite, a reminder about the Ask Me Anything sessions with SAP Champions, and exciting event recaps and announcements from Southern California, Cyprus, and Philadelphia!

This edition is filled with practical insights, innovation, and global community energy. Let's dive in!

Featured Blog Posts

Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule 

SAP expert Olga Dolinskaja, published a blog post about custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by Joule for Developers. Considering that the conversion system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is often a complex and time-consuming process. Olga, talks about how IT project managers face challenges defining scope, estimating effort, and understanding legacy code that often lacks documentation, while developers manually analyze ATC findings and interpret simplification notes. To address these challenges and accelerate migration projects, she explains how AI-based capabilities within SAP Joule for Developers now provide intelligent support for both managers and developers. Integrated into the Custom Code Migration app on SAP BTP ABAP environment and ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these capabilities offer Code Explain for understanding legacy logic and business purpose, ATC Explain for clear root-cause analysis of S/4HANA findings and much more. By reducing manual effort and providing contextual insights directly within development tools, these innovations help shorten migration timelines, enabling teams to meet deadlines and budget constraints more confidently. 

Read Olga blog post here 

Deep Dive into SAP Datasphere Object Store of BDC 

The HANA Data Lake Files (HDLF) service in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) Datasphere provides a cost-effective and scalable way to store and process large data volumes as Delta tables. SAP Community member, Sreekanth Surampally published a blog post to explain how inside BDC, SAP manages the Foundation Services (FOS) object store for standard SAP data products, while customers manage the Datasphere HDLF object store for their own SAP and non-SAP data. Through the document, he runs the details of building on hyperscale object storage and powered by Apache Spark, plus how HDLF enables large-scale replication and transformation flows, supporting medallion architecture (Bronze-Silver-Gold), and offering the "SQL on Files" feature to query Delta tables directly from HANA Cloud without loading data into memory. With images to illustrate the architecture and configurations, this is a must read if you are looking for depth and clarity about the topic. 

Read Sreekanth blog post here 

Joule A2A: Connect Code Based Agents into Joule 

SAP expert Felix Bartler, published valuable content about how to connect code based agents into Joule. Starting off considering that many organizations have already built custom AI agents using frameworks such as LangChain or LangGraph, his post demonstrates how such a custom-built agent can be integrated into the Joule ecosystem using the new pro-code extensibility features together with the A2A (Agent2Agent) protocol. A minimal ReAct-based currency conversion agent built in Python with LangGraph is exposed via an A2A server and deployed on SAP BTP. Using Joule capabilities defined in YAML (scenarios and functions), the agent is connected through an HTTP destination in SAP BTP and invoked via the new agent-request action type. Felix shows that the A2A protocol enables secure, structured communication between Joule and remote agents, while the Joule CLI supports compilation and deployment of the capability into a standalone or productive assistant.  

Read Felix blog post here 

UI for the BTP Application Vulnerability Report 

SAP Mentor Wouter Lemaire created a very useful blog post about the SAP BTP Application Vulnerability Report, which is a tool that scans your deployed applications for known vulnerabilities and exposes the findings through an API. To make this feature even better, he created an open-source UI I on top of the BTP Application Vulnerability Report API, to enable a better user experience to amplify and simplify the research. Running through the details of each step of his project, his solution enables sorting, filtering, free-text search, severity-based visual indicators, and Excel export of findings, making it easier to prioritize remediation and share reports with stakeholders. The project requires no database, as all data is fetched in real time from the API, and he also shared that the full source code is available on GitHub for contributions and enhancements. Try it out and give your feedback! 

Read Wouter blog post here 

Community News 

Hobbies Series: Ironman 

In this week's Hobbies Series, we are excited to share the inspiring story of Vinit Kumar, Development Architect in SAP Cloud Lifecycle Management (CLM), who discovered the world of triathlons in an unexpected way. In June 2014, Vinit suffered a serious accident, which left him with a fractured leg, a metal plate, and unable to walk for more than 30 minutes for over a year. Determined to recover, he began running as therapy and embracing the mindset that "pain will kill the pain." In 2017, he completed a half marathon despite still having implants, and in May 2019, he set his sights on Ironman 70.3 Goa, even without prior experience. Combining swimming, cycling, and running, the challenge became what he describes as a “teacher” about discipline, resilience, and the belief that limits are often self-imposed. Despite additional surgery and many problems on the preparation in Goa, Vinit kept repeating his mantra: "Pain is temporary, finishing is permanent." And in November 2022, after 7.5 hours across a 1.9 km sea swim, 90 km bike ride followed by a run, he proudly crossed the finish line. He also shared how triathlon mirrors life at SAP in many ways, like process integration and performance analytics to systems thinking, resilience, and change management. Proving that whether implementing SAP solutions or training for a race, the principle remains the same: all it takes is all you have got. 

Read Vinit blog post here 

Register to the new CEI project for SAP Integration Suite in 2026 

This year, the Global Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) for SAP Integration Suite is back! SAP expert, Alex Bundschuh, shared on his latest blog post the details about the project on gathering early feedback on product improvements, planned enhancements, and new developments, ensuring they align with real business needs. Following last year's strong participation of over 180 customers and partners from more than 20 countries, for 2026 the discussions will cover key topics such as SAP PI/PO migration, AI and generative AI, developer experience, and much more. Registration is open from February 9 to March 13, 2026, with monthly feedback sessions starting in April and running through the end of the year. If you are interested in taking part, and help shaping the SAP Integration Suite product, make sure to register here (link leads to the customer influence page).  

Read Alex blog post here 

REMINDER: Ask Me Anything Sessions with SAP Champions  

Last edition we mentioned already about our SAP Champion Sebastiano Marchesini initiative Ask Me Anything, but we would like to remind everyone that this first upcoming session will be open for questions only until the end of this week (February 28). So, don´t miss out in the opportunity on participating on real discussions and getting to have your questions answered by an expert. Each month, a different SAP Champion with a specific area of expertise will answer both technical and non-technical questions, submitted openly or anonymously. For this first edition, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Communities YouTube channel. For more details, make sure to read Sebastino full blog post bellow. 

Read Sebastiano blog post here 

Events 

SAP Southern California meetup recap 

This month was super exciting for the SAP Southern California local community, because they had their first meetup. Hosted by SAP Champion Vijayeta Sharma, the event brought together an inspiring group of professionals looking forward to connecting, exchanging ideas, and strengthening regional collaboration. The strong energy and enthusiasm set up a positive tone for future gatherings, with plans already underway for more meetups, knowledge-sharing sessions, and community-driven discussions. Participants are encouraged to share topic ideas and help shape events as the Southern California SAP network grows. So, if you are from the area or live close by, make sure to integrate into the SAP Community local group and participate in the next events.  

Read Vijayeta blog post here  

SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus recap 

In a recap shared by Vitaly Toraman, the third SAP Stammtisch in Cyprus once again brought together a vibrant group of SAP professionals, representing what Vitaly described as “nearly 100 years of combined SAP experience”. The session focused on S/4HANA transformation and highlighted five essential success factors: strong collaboration between business and IT, clear commitment and decision-making, true process simplification instead of lift-and-shift, structured change management, and early master data cleansing. The discussion underscored how valuable open exchange and local networking are in navigating complex transformation journeys, reinforcing the strength of the SAP Community in Cyprus (click here to join the group). So, if you are in the area, this is a perfect opportunity to connect with this incredible local community group. 

Read Vitaly blog post here 

SAP Inside Track Newtown Square, Philadelphia Summer 2026 

SAP Inside Track Philadelphia returns for its Summer 2026 edition! Planned for mid-June 2026 (9:00 AM - 4:00 PM EDT) at SAP America Headquarters in Newtown Square, PA (or a nearby location, as the organizing team are still planning the venue) this in-person event will once again bring together developers, partners, and SAP experts for a full day of knowledge sharing and networking. The 2026 agenda will focus on the next evolution of Business AI and Cloud ERP, covering topics such as Agentic AI, Joule integrations across SAP solutions, Clean Core strategies with RISE and GROW, and hands-on demos with SAP S/4HANA and Analytics. The organizers are currently building the agenda and invite community members interested in speaking to get involved. If you are in the area or would like to participate, the SAP community in Newtown Square (click here to join the group) is welcoming you. Mark your calendar and stay tuned for more details. 

Read Johna blog post here

entry.title: SAP-ABAP-1 retirement date
entry.content:
Dear all,

I was playing with the trail version of AI Launchpad. In the Model Library section there is the list of all available models. My attention was caught by SAP-ABAP-1 model. According to the official reference this model is a "pretrained foundation model that efficiently understands and explains ABAP code with immediate access to cloud compliant ABAP coding practices" .
So I thought Greate! I'd like to use it to build a service that generate ABAP documentation starting from legacy code ...
Unfortunately I discovered that this model will be retired on 2026-06-30 .

Therefore I was wondering why and if it will be replaced by another model.

entry.title: Re: Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1
entry.content:
Hi @JanMatthes,  

when @Stephan_Heinberg says, he would love to use it on S/4HANA On-Premise 2025, how can the answer be "it is available on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025"?

I am really interested in a honest explaination here. 

We as developers are sitting on On-Premise systems and want to use your new tooling (sidenote: Because it partly looks very promising!), but can't.

Your (official SAP) reply is to use Private Cloud. How? Do you think we can go to our stakeholders and convince them to sign a RISE contract just for the single reason, that we can use Joule for Developers? That does not happen - nowhere. The customer stays On-Premise and we as developers have to look about alternatives to use AI. This is happening right now. The ABAP community (outside SAP) is hardly discussing about J4D and it's features because hardly someone has access to it "in the real world".  

In addition, I also do not understand your recommendation to use BTP ABAP for side-by-side implementations for older On-Premise systems. Why? Just to be able to use Joule for Developers? There is a clear methodolody by SAP called SAP AEM to decide which way to go, when I want to extend something - and very often the best option is to extend a solution On-Stack using Developer Extensibility with ABAP Cloud in a clean-core-compliant manner. Should I ignore the methodology and architectural aspects just to be able to use Joule for developers during the implementation step on SAP BTP (not even mentoning additional infrastructure costs etc.) ?

I hope I did not offend you personally, but the ABAP community (outside SAP) is significantly annoyed about the lack of availability of Joule for Developers (ABAP AI capabilities) on On-Premise Systems in general - and this needs to be pointed out as much as possible.

It is really sad, that the potentially great features which are developed right now by SAP will never make it into the vast majority of the ABAP developers IDEs - just because of strategical decisions.

Best regards, 

Sebastian

entry.title: PR/PO Flexible Workflow gets stuck (SAP_WFRT)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'd like to request some guidence related to workflow approvals in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

We’re on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition with a customer using scope item BDK.

Flow: External system creates Sales Order → system creates Purchase Requisition → Purchase Order (Standard job) → a follow-up job creates Supplier Invoice and Billing Document (Custom jobs).

Problem: PR/PO flexible workflow / automatic approval is active in the tenant. If we deactivate it, some required custom logic stops working, so we must keep workflow active.

However, we frequently see workflow runtime issues where SAP_WFRT appears as processing user and the workflow instance becomes stuck/errored, which stops PR or PO processing and blocks the whole integration chain. If we go to Flexible Workflow Administration and Resume the workflow, everything continues normally. We've noticed that this happens mostly with sales orders with more than 2 items. We currently need the PR to PO conversion job (standard) to be per  item, meaning that if a PR has 3 items the standard job will create 3 PO´s and one of those usually gets stuck with the following error:

Then we need to manually resume the workflow for the whole process flow to work.

It's important to mention that we are currently using dev/cust and test environments.

Customer expects thousands of orders/day, so manual resume is not scalable/viable.

Questions:

  1. Is there a known root cause / fix for PR/PO workflows getting stuck with SAP_WFRT in Public Cloud?

  2. Is there any supported way to automate recovery, e.g., a scheduled job/template/API to detect + resume stuck workflow instances?

  3. If auto-resume isn’t supported, what’s SAP’s recommended approach for high-volume integration to avoid integration stoppage (monitoring/alerts/config patterns)?

Any pointers (SAP Notes/KBAs, configuration checks, best practices) would help.

 

Thank you in advance,

entry.title: Re: Unmanaged RAP — Deep Insert, CUD Operations & Action Handling
entry.content:
Hi @sanjay22 , 

Just delete  "strict ( 2 ); " from Behavior Definition and Behavior Projection. 

Spoiler

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_flightbooking unique;

//strict ( 2 );

 

define behavior for ZI_FlightBooking //alias <alias_name>

//late numbering

lock master

authorization master ( instance )

//etag master <field_name>

{

create ( authorization : global );

update;

delete;

field ( readonly : update ) booking_id;

association _Passengers { create; }

action ConfirmTicket result [1] $self;

}

 

define behavior for ZI_BookingPassenger //alias <alias_name>

//late numbering

lock dependent by _Flightbooking

authorization dependent by _Flightbooking

//etag master <field_name>

{

update;

delete;

field ( readonly ) booking_id;

field ( readonly : update ) passenger_no;

association _Flightbooking;

}

entry.title: Re: Pricing issue in the free goods delivery.
entry.content:
Dear @Amit66,

If the system is creating the free-goods subitem with item category TANN then free goods determination is working the remaining issue is pricing. In standard the TANN subitem is only free because the pricing procedure applies a 100% discount condition for free goods. If that condition is missing or not triggered the TANN line will keep a normal price and won’t be zero

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/c864b65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

What to check and typical root cause:

1. Open the Sales Order and select the TANN item

2. Go to Item Conditions and look for a 100% discount condition

    • If you do not see a 100% discount, the pricing procedure is the problem.
    • Note the Pricing Procedure name shown in analysis often a Y…

You are using a custom or country-specific pricing procedure that doesn’t contain or doesn’t trigger the 100% discount condition for free-goods items

Example (Italy): SAP documents that with pricing procedure Y23101 (Materials Italy) the 100% discount conditions are S100 / S200 (not D100). If those aren’t applied, TANN is valued instead of zero

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3483411

You fix this by adjusting/using the correct pricing procedure so the free-goods subitem gets the 100% discount:

In CBC:

  • Set Condition Types for Pricing SSCUI 101120 ensure the relevant 100% discount condition type exists/usable
  • Set Pricing Procedure SSCUI 101117 and
  • Set Pricing Procedure Determination SSCUI 101118 ensure your sales area/customer/document pricing procedure combination determines a pricing procedure that includes the free-goods 100% discount logic

More details below for example by clicking Open Documentation in CBC:

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How I Loaded 1.2 Billion Records into SAP HANA Without Crashing the Server (Or my Sanity)
entry.content:
@Tano_B  Thank you for the thoughtful questions — and that's a fascinating architecture with BW orchestrating through a virtual HANA table via DPA. I'd love to hear more about how that performed at scale, particularly how the orchestration layer handled failure recovery.

On the control table question: you're right that MAX(timestamp) inside HANA on a local columnar table is extremely fast — that's one of HANA's genuine strengths. The difference in our case came from the fact that we were calling MAX() against the virtual table, not a local one. Because the virtual table sits on top of a remote source via Smart Data Access, that MAX() call doesn't execute inside HANA's columnar engine — it gets pushed down to the remote source, which in our case was not optimized for that kind of aggregate scan across 1.2 billion rows. The round trip cost was significant and inconsistent depending on source system load at the time the procedure ran. Moving the boundary tracking to a local control table eliminated that dependency entirely and made execution time predictable rather than variable.

On the delta size question — this is an important distinction worth clarifying. The delta load does in fact scan across the full 1.2 billion rows on every run, and that is by design. We are not only capturing net-new records since the last load. We are also capturing updates and changes made to records that were already replicated to the target in previous runs.

A record that landed in the target six months ago could have been modified in the source system yesterday — and our delta needs to find it, identify that it changed, and apply the update to the target. That means the scan boundary cannot simply be "records created after timestamp X" — it has to evaluate the full dataset for any change_timestamp modification regardless of when the record was originally created.

This is what makes the partitioning and BIGINT decisions non-negotiable even for delta runs. You are not moving 2-8 million records in isolation — you are scanning 1.2 billion to find them. The arithmetic overhead and execution plan operate against the full table on every execution.

For use cases where source records are truly immutable after creation you could narrow the scan significantly and the architecture would look different. But for any source system where historical records get updated — which covers most ERP landscapes — the full scan on delta is the correct approach, not a limitation.

Really appreciate the questions.

entry.title: Re: Customer credit management issue
entry.content:
Dear @Amit66,

Why your first invoice can exceed:

This usually means your current setup is effectively checking only after FI open items exist and/or the SD documents are not updating credit exposure so the order value isn’t counted before billing

SAP own troubleshooting list for credit control not blocking points exactly to:

  • missing Static check of credit exposure (step 010) in the assigned check rule and/or
  • sales item category not marked Credit Active and/or
  • document type not correctly set for Automatic Credit Control / Credit Group

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3617102

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3367948

What to change to stop the first invoice:

Check 1.

In SSCUI 102623 Define Checking Rules ensure the check rule you actually use contains:

  • Step 010 Static/Statistical Check of Credit Exposure this is the key to stop first invoice slips through

SAP Notes below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3694862

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3617102

  • Optional but recommended Step 020 Maximum Document Value and set it to 50,000 if you want a hard stop for any single document above the limit

More details from SAP Community:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/credit-management-maximum-document-value/qaq-p/12381000

IMPORTANT: You generally cannot change SAP standard check rules 01/02/03

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3555470

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2880254

Check 2.

  • SSCUI 102137 Set Credit Limit Checks for Sales Document Types your order type must have Check Credit and a Credit Group assigned

SAP Note and additional information from SAP Community that can help below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3367948

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/understanding-sd-credit-management-integration-settings-for-troubleshooting/ba-p/12856200

  • SSCUI 101518 Define Automatic Credit Control maintain the combination Credit Segment + Risk Class + Credit Group with a reaction that blocks

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3363301

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3674047

Check 3.

If the item category isn’t credit active the order value may not count into exposure first billing can pass

  • SSCUI 102571 Determine Active Receivables Per Item Category tick Credit Active for the used item categories

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3617102

Summary:

Use Manage Credit Accounts ->Simulate Credit Check on the credit segment and simulate 60,000. It should trigger the intended block already at sales order stage

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/82af159fa1034d14851cea6a7b0e0c38.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/be5747120f22446dbaeabc02044722d3.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Customer credit management issue
entry.content: Hi! This is a common scenario in S/4HANA Cloud — the first invoice can bypass the limit. To block it, you’ll need to adjust your credit check configuration or risk category rules so that credit is checked at order creation, not just subsequent orders.

entry.title: Unexpected value in position currency
entry.content:
Hello,

we've migrated interest‑rate instruments denominated in EUR (local currency is PLN). In simple terms, these are the flows we manually entered in FTR_CREATE and fixed in TBB1_LC up to 31.12.2025.

TCODE:DATETRANSACTIONTRANS. CURRTRANS. AMOUNTVALUATION CURRVALUATION AMOUNTFX RATE:
TBB1_LC31.12.2020PRINCIPAL INCREASEEUR1 000 000,00PLN4 200 000,004,20000
TBB1_LC30.06.2022PARTIAL REPAYMENTEUR200 000,00PLN800 000,004,00000
TBB1_LC30.06.2022REALIZED FX DIFFEUR0PLN40 000,00 (200 000* 4,2 - 200 000 *4,0 ) 
GO-LIVE DATE01.01.2026NO ACTIONS IN SYSTEM     

Corresponding FX rates was entered on 31.12.2020 & 30.06.2022 into table TCURR thus in FTR_EDIT &TPM20  the flows look correct both in EUR and in PLN.  

We fixed all flows using TBB1_LC with up to 31.12.2025– and the system fixed everything correctly (the realized FX difference was also "posted" in TBB1_LC as a DBT). It's a simple instrument with only foreign currency valuation step in the position management procedure (no ammorization or security valuation).

However, when we try to run valuation in TPM1 for 31.01.2026, the system shows PLN amounts as if the realized FX difference never happened. In other words, we correctly have EUR = 800,000, but SAP shows the book value on 31.01.2026 in PLN value as 3,360,000 PLN, which is incorrect (it should be 4 200 000 - 800 000 = 3 400 000 PLN). The same is for TPM12 - BookValue seems to ignore that repayment was made at 4,0000 rate.

There are 3 lines in TPM13 on key date  = 30.06.2022.

Outgoing Payment - Position Outflow Installment Repayment to Be Paid (Liability)
Translation of Negative Purchase Value Derived business transaction
Forex Loss (Scheduled Outflow) Derived business transaction

Could you advise me what might be causing this issue?

entry.title: SELECT into TABLE and translate field contents
entry.content:
The following statement gets a certain DocEntry from the invoices (OINV) and joins it with a user defined value from the definition of PaymentGroups (OCTG):

SELECT T0."DocEntry", T0."GroupNum", T1."U_ShowPayment" from "OINV" T0
	LEFT JOIN "OCTG" T1 ON T1."GroupNum" = T0."GroupNum"
	where T0."DocEntry" = '18057';

That user-defined value is for example 'K';

a) how do I get this whole table into a variable?

I already have a procedure that can get the translation for this flag, so I can call

/* 9   = selected language, that is known
 * 'K' = the value in field 'U_ShowPayment'
 */
call mytrans('OCTG', 'ShowPayment', 'K', 9).

b) how can I change the 'K' in the resulting table for the value returned by mytrans() either directly in the select or afterwards in the resulting table?

entry.title: Re: Service Entry Sheet Performance period is not defaulted
entry.content:
Dear @Sudharmv,

What SAP confirms:

Since Public Cloud release 2308 (CE2308), if the PO has a single performance date (start = end) then in Manage Service Entry Sheets Lean Services the Performance Period facet shows only one date no from–to range. A range is shown only if a real range exists in the PO

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3365482

So in most cases the dates are copied but the UI intentionally displays them as one date when the PO doesn’t contain a true range

What to check in your PO:

Open the PO item and verify:

  • Start Date for Period of Performance
  • End Date for Period of Performance

If they are the same or effectively you maintained only one date the SES will show just one date by design

IMPORTANT: Even when you do have a range in the PO, SAP restricts the SES performance period to that PO range. If the actual service happened later the workaround is to update the PO performance period first then create/maintain the SES

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3655589

Summary:

If your PO item clearly has different start/end dates (a true range) and the SES performance period is still blank/not defaulted then it’s worth raising an SAP incident with PO+SES screenshots but that’s not what SAP Note 3365482 describes

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Performance Optimization for High-Concurrency
entry.content:
We are supporting a mid-sized law firm running SAP S/4HANA Cloud to manage finance, billing, and matter-related reporting.

In addition, the firm operates a public-facing website where clients can access case updates, invoices, and selected document insights via secure APIs exposed from S/4HANA. The workload is mostly read-heavy (case status, billing summaries, payment tracking).

During peak periods (e.g., major case announcements or month-end billing cycles), we observe Increased response time in custom OData services, Higher load on analytical CDS views and Noticeable database utilization spikes on SAP HANA

There are no dumps, but performance degradation impacts user experience both internally (Fiori users) and externally (client portal website).

I would appreciate expert guidance on the following:

In a Public Cloud setup, is it best practice to optimize primarily at CDS level (reducing joins, avoiding complex calculated fields), or should we redesign the architecture to decouple external read access?

For client-facing website integrations, would SAP recommend a side-by-side extension approach on SAP Business Technology Platform (e.g., CAP service with replicated data) to protect the S/4HANA core from traffic spikes?

Are there recommended patterns for handling high-concurrency read scenarios while maintaining clean core compliance?

Our objective is to maintain performance stability, protect transactional workloads, and stay aligned with SAP best practices for extensibility and clean architecture.

Looking forward to insights from those who have implemented similar external-facing integrations in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thank you.

entry.title: How to join a parameterized view?
entry.content:
The view:

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW "q_UFTrans"
(
	IN  TableName     NVARCHAR(30),
	IN  UserField     NVARCHAR(30),
	IN  LangInt       int
)

AS
	
	SELECT T0."FldValue", T3."Trans", T0."Descr"
		FROM          "UFD1" T0
			LEFT JOIN "CUFD" T1 ON (T0."FieldID" = T1."FieldID" AND T0."TableID" = T1."TableID")
			LEFT JOIN "OMLT" T2 ON T2."TableName" = 'UFD1'
			LEFT JOIN "MLT1" T3 ON T2."TranEntry" = T3."TranEntry" AND T3."LangCode" = :LangInt
		WHERE T0."TableID"  = :TableName;

It works:

However, if I try to join that in the following code....

	SELECT T0."DocEntry", T0."GroupNum", T1."U_ShowPayment", T2."Trans" from "OINV" T0
	LEFT JOIN "OCTG" T1 ON T1."GroupNum" = T0."GroupNum"
	LEFT JOIN "q_UFTrans"('OCTG','ShowPayment',9) T2 ON T2."FldValue" = T1."U_ShowPayment" 
	where T0."DocEntry" = '18057';

... I get the error:
SAP DBTech JDBC: [7]: feature not supported: Parameterized view with unused parameter: q_UFTrans

What is going on here? How do I join with a parameterized view?

entry.title: Re: How to get the URL of the OdataService generated by CDS
entry.content:
When you develop CDS views and OData services in SAP Business Application Studio, the HDI container created for your development branch is not exposed as a public endpoint. That’s why you can’t “find a URL” for your service inside your personal dev container — it simply isn’t reachable until you run it through a local CAP server or an approuter.

This is expected behavior. HDI containers are isolated by design.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud - Custom CDS View - Need Cred...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the credit limit you see in the Manage Credit Accounts app does not come from the classic FI‑AR tables. It is part of the SAP Credit Management (FIN-FSCM-CR) data model, which is separate from traditional AR master data. Because of this, the credit limit field is not exposed in the standard CDS views used for AR reporting, and it also does not appear in the Custom CDS View app by default.

entry.title: Cannot Select Today for New Schedule
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working with the Manage Work Center Utilization Fiori app (Resource Scheduling - RSH) and I've encountered an issue regarding the date selection while creating a new schedule.

 In the "Add to Schedule" pop-up, when I select "Create New Schedule", the system does not allow me to select the current date (today) as the start date. As seen in the attached screenshot, today is February 25th, 2026, but the calendar only allows selections starting from future dates. Is this a standard behavior where schedules can only be created for future periods rather than the current day?

I have heard that this can be resolved through enhancement, but I am concerned about how this would affect Freeze and Execution phase logic. (Schedule is deleted when period is started if its not frozen)

 

Also in the Generate Recurring Schedules app, the system allows selecting 'Today' as the start date with single,  creates a schedule but it fails to show in manage schedule app;

entry.title: Re: clean core alternative to BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE?
entry.content: @sh4il3sh  to my best understanding, as of now there is currently no released CDS view or RAP BO in S/4HANA that provides full replacement functionality for BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE.

entry.title: Re: Text in GOS note popup not cleared after calling cl_gos_document_service->edit_note
entry.content:
This is known limitation of the GOS note editor when it is called repeatedly in the same ABAP session.
The class CL_GOS_DOCUMENT_SERVICE internally caches the note editor instance, so when you call EDIT_NOTE multiple times, the previous text buffer is not cleared. As a result, the popup keeps showing the text from the first call. it’s how the GOS editor is implemented.

One thing you can try, it may work, If you wrap the GOS call in a separate transaction, try and revert in case if it work.

something like  " CALL FUNCTION 'Your_GOS_EDITOR_FM' STARTING NEW TASK .

entry.title: Service Layer endpoint /b1s/v2/Attachments2 - BadFormat error
entry.content:
Hi,

I'am trying to upload a attachment unsing Java Apache HTTP Client version 5 and keep getting the folwowing error:

{
   "error" : {
      "code" : "-1",
      "message" : "BadFormat"
   }
}

Can someone help me find what I could be missing on this test class:

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

import java.io.File;
import java.io.FileInputStream;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.security.KeyManagementException;
import java.security.KeyStoreException;
import java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;
import java.security.UnrecoverableKeyException;
import java.security.cert.CertificateException;
import java.time.Instant;
import java.util.Objects;

import javax.net.ssl.SSLContext;

import org.apache.commons.io.IOUtils;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.classic.methods.HttpGet;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.classic.methods.HttpPost;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.entity.mime.HttpMultipartMode;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.entity.mime.MultipartEntityBuilder;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.impl.classic.CloseableHttpClient;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.impl.classic.HttpClientBuilder;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.impl.io.PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.impl.io.PoolingHttpClientConnectionManagerBuilder;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.routing.RoutingSupport;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.ssl.DefaultClientTlsStrategy;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.ssl.HostnameVerificationPolicy;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.ssl.NoopHostnameVerifier;
import org.apache.hc.client5.http.ssl.TlsSocketStrategy;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.ClassicHttpResponse;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.ContentType;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.Header;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.HttpEntity;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.HttpHost;
import org.apache.hc.core5.http.io.entity.EntityUtils;
import org.apache.hc.core5.ssl.SSLContexts;
import org.apache.hc.core5.ssl.TrustStrategy;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;

class TestAttachments2
{
	protected static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(TestAttachments2.class);
	
    private static final String TARGET_URL = "https://127.0.0.1:90000/b1s/v2/Attachments2";
    
    @test
    void testUpload()
    {
    	final Instant startInstant = Instant.now();
    	
        try
        {
			final HttpGet httpGet = new HttpGet(TARGET_URL);
			String name;
			
			name = "/tmp/test.pdf";
			
			File f = new File(name);
			
			byte[] byteArray = IOUtils.toByteArray(new FileInputStream(name));
			
			final HttpHost host = RoutingSupport.determineHost(httpGet);
			
			final MultipartEntityBuilder builder = MultipartEntityBuilder.create();
			builder.setMode(HttpMultipartMode.LEGACY);
			builder.addBinaryBody("file", byteArray, ContentType.APPLICATION_PDF, f.getName());
			
			final HttpEntity entity = builder.build();
			
			final HttpPost post = new HttpPost(TARGET_URL);
			
			post.setHeader("Accept", "*/*");
			post.setHeader("Cookie", "ROUTEID=.node3; B1SESSION=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx");
			post.setHeader("Authorization", "Bearer xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx");
			
			post.setEntity(entity);
			
			try( CloseableHttpClient client = buildClient())
			{
				final ClassicHttpResponse response = client.executeOpen(host, post, null);
				logger.info("response.getCode(): {}", response.getCode());
				
				final HttpEntity repoEntity = response.getEntity();
				
				if (Objects.nonNull(repoEntity))
				{
					logger.info("\n\n{}\n\n", EntityUtils.toString(repoEntity));
				}
				
				if (response.getHeaders() != null)
				{
					for (Header item : response.getHeaders())
					{
						logger.info(item.getName() + ": " + item.getValue());
					}
				}
			}
			
			
        }
        catch (Exception e)
        {
        	logger.error(e.getMessage(), e);
        }
		
		assertTrue( startInstant.isBefore(Instant.now()) );
    }
    
    private CloseableHttpClient buildClient() throws KeyManagementException, UnrecoverableKeyException, NoSuchAlgorithmException, KeyStoreException, CertificateException, IOException
    {
		final TrustStrategy acceptAllCerts = (cert, authType) -> true;
		
		final SSLContext sslcontext = SSLContexts.custom()
				.loadTrustMaterial(acceptAllCerts)
				.build();

		final DefaultClientTlsStrategy tlsStrategy = new DefaultClientTlsStrategy(sslcontext,
				HostnameVerificationPolicy.CLIENT, NoopHostnameVerifier.INSTANCE);
		
		final PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager connManager = PoolingHttpClientConnectionManagerBuilder.create()
				.setTlsSocketStrategy((TlsSocketStrategy) tlsStrategy)
				.build();
		
		final CloseableHttpClient client = HttpClientBuilder.create()
		        .setConnectionManager(connManager)
		        .build();
		
		return client;
    }
}

 I use Sl4J and Logback for logging and bellow are the XML to configure the logging framework:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration>
	<include resource="org/springframework/boot/logging/logback/defaults.xml"/>
	
	<statusListener class="ch.qos.logback.core.status.NopStatusListener" />
	​
	<springProperty scope="context" name="springAppName" source="spring.application.name"/>
	<!-- Example for logging into the build folder of your project -->
	<property name="LOG_FILE" value="${BUILD_FOLDER:-build}/${springAppName}"/>​

	<!-- Appender to log to console -->
	<appender name="console" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
		<filter class="ch.qos.logback.classic.filter.ThresholdFilter">
			<!-- Minimum logging level to be presented in the console logs-->
			<level>DEBUG</level>
		</filter>
		<encoder>
			<pattern>%d{dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%X{X-Trace-Id}] [%thread] %-5level %logger{36}.%M:%line - %msg%n</pattern>
			<charset>utf8</charset>
		</encoder>
	</appender>
	
	<logger name="org.apache.http" level="DEBUG"/>
	<logger name="org.apache.hc.client5.http" level="debug"/>
	​
	<root level="INFO">
		<appender-ref ref="console"/>
	</root>
</configuration>

entry.title: AI-Powered Resume Assessment

The Future of Smarter, Faster Talent Screening

Finding top talent shouldn’t feel like searching for a needle in a haystack. Yet manual resume reviews are slow, inconsistent, and often influenced by unconscious bias.

It’s time for a smarter approach.

Join our upcoming lab session, AI-Powered Resume Assessment: Why It’s a Must-Have for Every Program, designed exclusively for SAP Fieldglass users ready to modernize their workforce screening.

Register now and take the first step toward smarter workforce management:
https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5234455/09D6931769E691DAE81020D168F92A74

Get step-by-step guidance from experienced SAP Fieldglass specialists who’ve successfully implemented AI-powered resume screening across industries. Avoid common pitfalls and fast-track your deployment with proven best practices.

⚡Boost Efficiency & Quality

Discover how AI-driven scoring:

  • Speeds up resume screening

Walk away with real metrics and insights you can apply immediately.

📚What You’ll Learn

Especially valuable for:

entry.title: Re: Select by default 3 checkboxes in the class cancellat...
entry.content:
Hi @peter_schwartz3 

Currently, there is no way to configure which options should be selected by default at the Cancel Class wizard > Step 3: Post Cancellation Action Settings.

There have been some enhancements requests on it, and our customers are welcomed to vote for any open requests and/or create additional ones:

  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/328886
  • https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/211647 

For more details on enhancement requests, please check the KBA 2090228 - How to submit enhancement ideas for SAP SuccessFactors Products.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: ADT Code Completion is (Still) Slow
entry.content:
Hello cafeteria_fraiche,

I see that the code completion in ADT Eclipse is slower than in SAP GUI SE38. In ADT Eclipse we have more frameworks in use than in SAP GUI stack.

Unfortunately we cannot improve the performance to a point where is as fast as in SAP GUI.
On the other hand the code completion in ADT Eclipse can also perform using wildcards, for example "obj->get*name" which may present proposals such as "get_name" and "get_surname" and "get_middle_name".

Regards,
Ünal
